obligations.reason: The fact layer exposes external JTAG ports and DMI/debug modules with outputs debug_req_o, haltreq_o, resumereq_o, dmactive_o, and a jtag_key input. Analyze whether external debug operations are actually gated by authentication/authorization rather than only decoded by the TAP/DMI path.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DMI interface
obligations.object: hart debug control state and CPU execution control
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI requests that can halt/resume harts or enter debug mode should be mediated by debug authorization/authentication such as jtag_key match, debug enable/lifecycle state, or equivalent access control before asserting hart debug controls.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG pins/tap -> dmi_jtag -> dmi_cdc -> dm_top/dm_csrs -> debug_req_o/haltreq_o/resumereq_o
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive state required before debug control assertions
jtag_key comparison or equivalent authorization state
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: jtag_key or debug authorization/lifecycle enable
path from pins to control outputs
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer lists jtag_key and debug outputs but does not show assignments; inspect RTL to determine whether the key is enforced and where.

obligations.reason: dm_csrs exposes sbaddress, sbdata, sbreadonaddr, sbreadondata, sbdata_write_valid, and dm_top instantiates dm_sba and AXI slave/master connectivity. Check whether the debug path can access protected address ranges without explicit authorization or range mediation.
obligations.subject: debug module system bus access master
obligations.operation: read/write system bus via debug module
obligations.object: system memory and memory-mapped peripherals reachable through debug SBA/AXI
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-originated system bus reads/writes should be constrained by debug authorization and any system address/PMP/range policy applicable to protected memory/peripherals; DMI CSR writes that launch SBA transfers should not bypass normal fabric permissions.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DMI -> dm_csrs system bus access controls -> dm_top i_dm_sba -> AXI master/fabric
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug auth plus address/range policy
obligations.uncertainty: The included facts identify SBA signals and instance but not the internals of dm_sba; inspect visible connections and any available module body to establish reachability and guards.

obligations.reason: The debug CSR module exposes ndmreset_o and dmactive_o to top-level integration. Because reset is a privileged control effect, inspect whether DMI writes to these controls are properly authorized and reset-safe.
obligations.subject: external debug requester
obligations.object: global/non-debug reset and debug module active state
obligations.expected_guard: Writes that assert non-debug-module reset (ndmreset) or activate/deactivate the debug module should require authorized DMI access and should have safe reset defaults; reset control should not be reachable from unauthenticated/test paths.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI request decode/write cases in dm_csrs -> dmactive_o/ndmreset_o outputs
required_evidence_slots: object: ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and downstream reset tree
expected guard: debug authorization plus valid DMI CSR write
obligations.uncertainty: Assignments to ndmreset_o/dmactive_o are not visible in the fact summary; analyze dm_csrs write cases and integration.

obligations.reason: The design has DMI response paths to JTAG and dm_csrs inputs for sbdata_i and data_valid_i. Verify that read muxes and response registers do not leak protected data without authorization or after reset/deactivation/error states.
obligations.subject: external JTAG reader
obligations.object: DMI-readable debug CSR contents, system bus read data, abstract command data
obligations.expected_guard: Data returned over DMI/JTAG should be limited to authorized debug sessions and should not expose protected internal state, bus read data, or stale response data after reset/deactivation/error.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI request/response CSR read mux in dm_csrs -> sbdata_i/data_i/hart status/abstract data -> dmi_jtag td_o
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: authorized debug access and valid read response
obligations.uncertainty: Protected nature of specific DMI data depends on decoded addresses and bus targets; use RTL to identify data sources and valid gating.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile has csr_addr_i, csr_wdata_i, csr_rdata_o, fu_op, umode_i, debug_req_i, and many security-relevant outputs. Inspect decode/write paths to ensure privileged CSRs are not writable/readable from user mode or inappropriate debug state.
obligations.subject: software CSR interface / CPU privilege mode
obligations.object: privileged CSRs and outputs including satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o, debug_mode_o, single_step_o, cache enables, perf interface
obligations.expected_guard: Writes and reads of privileged CSRs that control address translation, privilege status, trap vectors, debug mode, cache enables, or performance interfaces should be gated by current privilege/debug state; user-mode access should be denied or faulted.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile CSR write/read decode -> status/satp/translation/debug/performance outputs
state_conditions_to_check: exception handling for illegal CSR access
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: privilege check and/or debug-mode check
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not include internal privilege-check expressions; inspect all CSR case statements around listed lines.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile accepts debug_req_i and emits debug_mode_o, set_debug_pc_o, single_step_o, and halt_csr_o. Check that debug-mode transitions and debug-only controls are mediated by valid external debug authorization and not writable from normal/unprivileged CSR paths.
obligations.subject: debug module and software CSR interface
obligations.object: core debug mode state, debug PC redirection, single-step control, halt CSR behavior
obligations.expected_guard: Entry to and control of debug mode should require a valid debug request source and should not be triggerable by ordinary CSR writes or unprivileged state transitions; debug-only CSRs should be accessible only while in debug mode.
required_evidence_slots: subject: debug request source and CSR writer
obligations.uncertainty: Need cross-module trace from dm_csrs haltreq/debug_req to csr_regfile debug_req_i plus internal CSR checks.

obligations.reason: dmi_jtag_tap has testmode_i and DFT clock mux instance, and outputs dmi_access_o, dmi_reset_o, tdo_oe_o. Inspect whether testmode can bypass normal TAP/debug authorization or expose TDO/DMI paths.
obligations.subject: test/DFT and JTAG interface
obligations.operation: enable/route DMI or reset through test/TAP logic
obligations.object: DMI access selection, DMI reset, TDO output enable
obligations.expected_guard: Testmode or DFT clock muxing should not enable DMI access, reset override, or TDO drive unless the same debug authorization and TAP state rules are satisfied; test-only controls should be isolated from protected debug operations in normal operation.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag_tap testmode_i/clock mux and TAP state -> dmi_access_o/dtmcs_select_o/dmi_reset_o
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: normal-mode isolation plus debug authorization
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies ports and clock mux but not the selected behavior; inspect TAP state machine and mux use.

obligations.reason: rom2 exposes req_i, we_i, addr_i, wdata_i, rdata_o despite being named ROM and is instantiated in peripheral address space. Check whether we_i is ignored, permanently disabled, or protected by an initialization/test guard.
obligations.subject: bus/MMIO requester
obligations.object: rom2/boot ROM contents and read data
obligations.expected_guard: ROM/boot code regions should reject writes or require a secure provisioning/lifecycle guard; if a write port exists, writes must be disabled or constrained to authorized initialization/test conditions.
obligations.candidate_path: rom2/APB/register interface -> req_i,we_i,addr_i,wdata_i -> rom2 storage/read data
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: no writes or secure init/test guard
observed write path and address decode
obligations.uncertainty: rom2 body is small but facts do not show its assignments; inspect write handling and instantiation wiring.

obligations.reason: ariane_xilinx instantiates xdma_0 and AXI clock/data width converters. Since PCIe is an external bus master, analyze whether its AXI path is range-limited or can access protected CPU memory/peripherals without mediation.
obligations.subject: PCIe XDMA external master
obligations.object: DDR/system memory and memory-mapped peripherals reachable from PCIe XDMA
obligations.expected_guard: External DMA/PCIe masters should be restricted by an address map/IOMMU/PMP-like range check or fabric firewall before reaching protected memory or MMIO; width/clock converters must preserve address/control attributes.
obligations.candidate_path: external PCIe XDMA/AXI master -> width/clock converters -> AXI fabric/memory/peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: converter connectivity and control signal preservation
obligations.uncertainty: Only top-level instances are listed; inspect connections in ariane_xilinx to identify reachable ranges and any guards.

obligations.reason: serpent_peripherals exposes distinct NoC channels for ariane_debug, bootrom, clint, and plic plus debug control outputs. Check that routing and address decoding preserve intended permissions and do not let general NoC traffic drive privileged debug/reset/interrupt controls.
obligations.subject: OpenPiton NoC requester
obligations.object: debug module, bootrom, CLINT timer/IPI controls, PLIC interrupt controls
obligations.expected_guard: NoC request channels targeting debug, bootrom, CLINT, and PLIC should enforce requester/address permissions and should not allow debug or control peripherals to be reached by unauthorized NoC traffic.
obligations.candidate_path: openpiton serpent_peripherals NoC debug/bootrom/clint/plic ports -> local peripheral wrappers and DMI/JTAG
required_evidence_slots: object: debug/bootrom/CLINT/PLIC registers or control outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists ports but not internal routing; inspect serpent_peripherals assignments and wrapper instances.
